title: Field-Relative vs Robot-Relative Driving
subtitle: The same joystick push means different things depending on whether commands are interpreted from the field or the robot.
summary: The robotics writing describes gyro-dependent swerve behavior and why orientation sensing matters.

artifact.title: Command frame changes module vectors
artifact.description: A swerve command combines translation and rotation, but the frame of reference decides what forward means.

sections.heading: Robot-Relative
body: Forward means the direction the robot is currently facing. If the robot turns, the same joystick input moves in a new field direction.

sections.heading: Field-Relative
body: Forward means the field's forward direction. The robot uses its gyro heading to rotate the driver command into robot coordinates.
sections.callout: Field-relative driving feels natural to drivers, but it depends on trustworthy heading estimation.

pitfalls: Using the wrong coordinate convention in kinematics.
Not resetting gyro heading before a match.
Failing gracefully when the gyro disconnects.

quiz.question: What sensor is usually essential for field-relative driving?
quiz.explanation: The robot needs heading to rotate field commands into robot-relative commands.
